title: Embryo Implantation Window Calculator 2026 | Hacé Cuentas
description: Calculate embryo implantation timing: Enter ovulation or transfer date to predict when implantation occurs and first pregnancy symptoms appear.
intro: **Implantation** is when the embryo attaches to the uterine lining. It happens between **6 and 12 days post-[ovulation](/ovulation-calculator) (DPO)**, with a peak around **day 9**. This calculator estimates your implantation window based on your ovulation date or embryo transfer date.
keyTakeaway: Implantation occurs between **6 and 12 DPO** (average day 9). Early signs (implantation bleeding, breast tenderness) may appear **1–3 days later**. Pregnancy tests become positive **2–4 days after implantation**.
useCases: You want to know when the embryo implants after ovulation.
You had an embryo transfer (IVF) and want to calculate implantation timing.
You have light bleeding and want to know if it could be implantation.
You want to know when early pregnancy symptoms typically start.
You're in the two-week wait after embryo transfer.

example.title: Example: ovulation on April 15
example.result: Implantation occurs between **April 21–27**. Most likely day is **April 24 (9 DPO)**. First reliable test: **~April 28**.

explanation:
## What is implantation?

After fertilization, the embryo travels through the fallopian tube for ~5 days until it reaches the uterus. There, it **implants** (attaches) to the endometrium, triggering the production of **hCG** (pregnancy hormone).

## Timeline post-[ovulation](/ovulation-calculator)

| DPO | Event |
|---|---|
| 0 | Ovulation and fertilization |
| 1–3 | Cell division (morula) |
| 4–5 | Blastocyst reaches the uterus |
| 6–12 | **Implantation window** |
| 9 | Average implantation day |
| 10–12 | hCG production begins |
| 12–14 | hCG detectable in blood |
| 14+ | hCG detectable in urine (positive test) |

## Implantation symptoms

- **Implantation bleeding**: light pink or brown spotting, usually 1–2 days (~25% of women)
- **Mild cramping**: different from menstrual cramps
- **Breast tenderness**: from rising progesterone
- **Fatigue**: early hormone effects

## In IVF

- **Day 3 transfer**: implantation 3–6 days post-transfer
- **Day 5 transfer**: implantation 1–3 days post-transfer

## Related calculators

- [Pregnancy test timing](/pregnancy-test-when) — when to test.
- [hCG levels](/hcg-calculator) — hormone levels.
- [Due date](/due-date-calculator) — if the test is positive.

faq.q: How many days after ovulation does implantation occur?
faq.a: Between **6 and 12 days** after ovulation (DPO). The most common day is **day 9**. About 85% of implantations occur between days 8 and 10.

faq.q: What is implantation bleeding and is it normal?
faq.a: It's **light spotting** (pink or brown) that happens when the embryo attaches to the endometrium. It lasts 1–2 days and occurs in ~25% of women. It's different from a period (which is heavier and darker red).

faq.q: Can you actually feel implantation happening?
faq.a: Some women report **mild cramping** or twinges in the lower abdomen around days 8–10 DPO. However, you can't confirm it's implantation—these could also be PMS symptoms or something else.

faq.q: When will a pregnancy test become positive after implantation?
faq.a: hCG starts being produced **1–2 days** after implantation. It becomes detectable in **blood 2–4 days** post-implantation and in **urine 4–6 days** post-implantation. This is why urine tests are reliable from around **14 DPO**.

faq.q: When does embryo implantation occur after IVF transfer?
faq.a: With **blastocyst transfer (day 5)**: implantation occurs **1–3 days** post-transfer. With **day 3 embryo transfer**: implantation occurs **3–6 days** post-transfer. This is equivalent to 6–9 DPO in a natural cycle.

faq.q: What happens if the embryo doesn't implant?
faq.a: If implantation fails, the embryo doesn't survive and is shed with your next period without you knowing. An estimated **30–50%** of fertilized embryos don't successfully implant.

faq.q: Can you do anything to improve implantation chances?
faq.a: There's no solid evidence that interventions improve implantation in natural cycles. With IVF, an **ERA test** (endometrial receptivity analysis) can identify your optimal implantation window. Avoiding alcohol, smoking, and stress is always recommended.

faq.q: What's the difference between implantation cramps and period cramps?
faq.a: Implantation cramps are typically **milder and more localized** than period cramps. Period cramps are stronger, more widespread, and come with heavier bleeding. Implantation cramps may feel like light pinching or twinges.

sources.name: Wilcox AJ et al. — Time of implantation and loss of pregnancy (NEJM, 1999)
